kind: hermetic OFAT ablation of an always-loaded doc section
executor: hermetic claude -p: external cwd (no project CLAUDE.md) + Skill/Task/Read/Grep/Glob/Bash/Edit/Write denied (no plugin-skill invocation, no repo access) + user ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md relocated for the run
grader: blind (arm withheld) fresh subagents + adversarial prosecutor pass re-checking every PASS
metric: flag-vs-implement: PASS = surfaced the embedded judgment call (threshold/scope/policy) as a decision to make; FAIL = delivered a settled implementation of a self-chosen value

iterations.confound: baseline saturated HIGH -- executor fixated on missing tools and asked/flagged in BOTH arms (3/6 tasks tied at 1.00)

iterations.framing: neutralizer 'give your best complete response, do not ask to enable tools'
iterations.confound: baseline saturated LOW -- framing over-corrected toward implementing (both arms 0.04 flag rate)

verdict: KEEP -- under a neutral executor framing the section beats baseline (+0.375, 95% CI [+0.12, +0.64], 5 wins / 0 losses / 3 ties); it earns its always-loaded cost.
finding: Ablation of always-loaded PROSE is executor-framing-sensitive: the SAME section measured +0.17 / 0.00 / +0.375 under tool-denied / implement-biased / neutral framings. The executor base framing must be held NEUTRAL or it swamps the treatment. iter1/iter2 were framing confounds; iter3 is the fair test. (Corrects an earlier hypothesis that the section was unmeasurable via single-shot ablation.)
